name: Beef Pot Roast (Pressure Cooker)
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds boneless brisket of beef, uniformly shaped
2 cups canned or fresh beef broth
recipeInstructions:
1. In the pressure cooker, brown the brisket in hot oil on all sides evenly, turning with a long-handled fork or tongs.
2. Remove the meat and set aside.
3. Saute onion, garlic, and sliced carrots in hot oil for 3 minutes, stirring and scraping the bottom of the cooker to loosen any browned particles of meat.
4. Stir in broth, lemon juice, tomato paste, parsley, and seasonings.
5. Place the rack in the cooker; add the meat. Secure the lid.
6. Bring the cooker to high pressure over high heat. Reduce the heat to medium and cook for 50-55 minutes.
7. Release steam according to manufacturer's directions. Remove the lid.
8. Stir potatoes and carrots into the meat mixture. Secure the lid.
9. Bring the cooker to high pressure over high heat. Reduce the heat to medium and cook for 9 minutes.
10. Release steam according to manufacturer's directions. Remove the lid.
11. Transfer the meat and vegetables to a serving platter. Loosely cover to retain heat.
12. Skim all but approximately 2 tablespoons of liquid from the cooker; strain this liquid through a fine sieve and set aside.
13. Heat the cooker over low heat; stir in flour to make a smooth paste.
14. Add the strained liquid; stir continuously until the sauce is smooth and thickened, about 5-7 minutes.
15. Remove the skillet from the heat; whisk in sour cream and horseradish until well blended.
16. Slice the brisket. Serve the sauce with brisket and vegetables.
NOTE: This dish can be made very flavorful without the horseradish sauce and is excellent served with a simple salad.
VARIATION: Instead of making the horseradish sauce, add 1 teaspoon oregano, 1 teaspoon thyme, 1 teaspoon basil, 1/2 teaspoon rosemary -OR- 1 tablespoon Italian seasoning to the cooker with the tomato paste. You may also substitute 1 cup red wine (do not use cooking wine; use a nice table wine) or beer for 1 cup of the beef broth.
